reforms.description: The introduction of the 'brownfield' programme aimed to reduce the burden of potential Superfund liability on developers of contaminated land, facilitating urban regeneration in poorer districts.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By alleviating liability concerns, the programme encourages investment in urban areas, potentially leading to economic revitalization and increased property values.
reforms.status_evidence: Some regeneration projects have subsequently succeeded, though take-up has been less than hoped.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to address urban decay and promote sustainable development, although its impact has been limited.
reforms.source_quote: The 'brownfield' programme was thought to be a factor impeding urban regeneration, particularly in poorer districts.
